title: Legal Discovery Intelligence Graph
summary: A deployed Graph RAG investigation workspace with cited evidence, entity graphs, and reproducible evaluation.
outcome: A public synthetic-matter investigation workflow combines vector and graph retrieval, cited evidence, timelines, privilege/PII flags, calibrated refusal, and an auditable case brief.

deployment.runtime: Flask + gunicorn with an ONNX embedding backend

metrics: Entity-mention extraction micro F1 0.887 strict on the 455-document synthetic corpus
Hybrid retrieval R@10 0.857 on the committed dense-corpus evaluation
Relationship-query hit@5 improved from 0.500 vector-only to 0.833 with graph expansion
Privilege and synthetic-PII flag F1 1.0 on clean templated text
stages: Deterministic synthetic corpus and gold-label generation
Real-file ingestion for PDF, DOCX, and EML
Entity, event, privilege, and synthetic-PII extraction
PostgreSQL pgvector indexing and Neo4j graph loading
Hybrid retrieval with calibrated refusal and cited evidence
Flask investigation UI with graph, timeline, evaluation, and case brief
architecture: Synthetic discovery corpus and gold labels
Hybrid evidence retriever and refusal policy
evaluation: artifacts/extraction_metrics.json records strict and relaxed span-matching metrics against emitted gold labels
artifacts/retrieval_metrics.json records vector-only and graph-expanded retrieval by query category and k
artifacts/flags_metrics.json records document-level privilege and synthetic-PII rule results
The repository documents the dense-corpus regressions and warns that templated synthetic text overstates real-world performance
operationalTradeoffs: Graph expansion materially improves relationship hit@5 but can dilute some dense-corpus results at larger k
The Render free tier sleeps, has a constrained memory envelope, and requires an ONNX backend rather than torch
The public app is deliberately read-only and synthetic; real eDiscovery would require access control, matter isolation, OCR, retention, and legal/privacy review
A public HTTP response is verified, but this is not an availability, confidentiality, or production-SLO claim
disclaimer: All documents, people, companies, amounts, identifiers, and events are synthetic and fictional. Metrics are reproducible results on templated synthetic text and are not claims about real legal matters, privilege determinations, or production discovery performance.

dataDisclosure.source: Repository-generated Project Falcon synthetic legal-discovery corpus, seed 42
dataDisclosure.license: Repository project license; no third-party client or matter data
dataDisclosure.permittedUse: Portfolio demonstration, deterministic evaluation, and engineering research
includedFields: Fictional documents, entities, events, amounts, relationships, and planted evidence
Synthetic privilege and PII-like labels used solely for evaluation
excludedFields: Real client, employee, custodian, matter, communication, and personal data
Production legal advice, privilege determinations, and discovery decisions
deployedArtifactContents: Synthetic corpus-derived indexes and graph records
Read-only Flask application and committed aggregate evaluation artifacts
No real discovery files or client data

evidence.claim: Entity-mention extraction achieved micro F1 0.887 strict on the committed synthetic corpus.
evidence.method: One-to-one strict span matching against generator-emitted gold labels

evidence.claim: Hybrid retrieval achieved R@10 0.857 and graph expansion improved relationship hit@5 to 0.833.
evidence.method: Versioned labeled-query evaluation comparing vector-only and graph-expanded retrieval

evidence.claim: Privilege and synthetic-PII rules achieved F1 1.0 on clean templated text.
evidence.method: Document-level rule evaluation against synthetic gold labels

evidence.claim: The current Render application root returned HTTP 200.
evidence.method: Read-only HTTP request with redirects followed; no user data submitted

evidence.claim: The corpus, gold labels, people, organizations, identifiers, and events are generated and fictional.
evidence.method: Versioned generator and data/evaluation documentation review

story.recruiterSummary: Legal discovery teams need to connect people, events, documents, and evidence without losing the source trail. This project demonstrates that workflow on a fully fictional matter: reviewers can search, traverse relationships, inspect a timeline, and open cited documents while the product clearly distinguishes evidence from generated structure.
story.technicalNarrative: A deterministic generator emits a 455-document corpus and gold labels. Offline pipelines extract entities/events, create pgvector embeddings, and load Neo4j relationships. The deployed Flask app uses an ONNX embedding backend within Render's memory limit, fuses vector and graph retrieval, applies a calibrated refusal threshold, and renders cited results, graphs, timelines, flags, and evaluation artifacts.
scalabilityRoadmap: Add authenticated matter isolation, role-based access, audit retention, and encrypted document storage
Introduce OCR and robust parsing for scans, forwarded chains, and production document variance
Move ingestion and indexing to background workers with versioned index promotion
Add real-world benchmark sets under appropriate legal/data agreements and revalidate thresholds
Retire free-tier keep-alive behavior under an owner-approved paid reliability envelope
story.executiveSummary: A public, evidence-first Graph RAG case workspace built on a reproducible synthetic legal matter.
story.intendedUser: A legal-technology reviewer or discovery analyst evaluating evidence retrieval and relationship navigation.

example.title: Investigate a relationship while retaining citations
steps: Open the fictional matter and choose a guided investigation question
Review hybrid search results and the calibrated evidence/refusal state
Traverse related entities and events in the graph and timeline
Open cited synthetic documents and compare the result with committed evaluation

technologyDecisions.rationale: Vector similarity and explicit relationship traversal serve complementary discovery questions.
technologyDecisions.tradeoff: Two stores add operational complexity, and graph expansion requires category-level regression measurement.

technologyDecisions.rationale: Fits the embedding model inside the free-tier memory envelope while preserving measured vector parity.
technologyDecisions.tradeoff: Requires an exported model and parity testing but avoids the deployed worker's torch memory failure.

limitations: Synthetic templated text is materially cleaner than real discovery data.
The public demo has no real-client security or matter-isolation posture.
Free-tier services can sleep or pause and do not establish an availability SLO.
Privilege/PII flags are research aids, not legal or privacy determinations.

bulletCandidates.bullet: Built a deployed Graph RAG investigation workspace with Flask, pgvector, Neo4j, ONNX embeddings, cited evidence, and calibrated refusal, achieving 0.887 strict entity F1 on a 455-document synthetic corpus.
Evaluated vector and graph-expanded retrieval by query category, improving relationship-query hit@5 from 0.500 to 0.833 while documenting dense-corpus tradeoffs.
